title: Real Estate Transaction Coordinator / Listing Coordinator (2)
description:
What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM! 

Why should you partner with us? WE ARE NOT YOUR ORDINARY CLIENT! (aka #NotYourOrdinaryAFAM lol) 



We’re hiring a highly organized, systems-minded Real Estate Transaction Coordinator.

This role is for someone who lives in the details, obsesses over contract deadlines, and can manage a complex transaction pipeline without a single ball being dropped. You aren't just "checking boxes"—you proactively identifies gaps in the contract-to-close process, automates repetitive tasks, and ensures our clients and partners experience a seamless, "magic 
nt" closing every single time.



IMPORTANT: Please read the entire job listing carefully. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



ABOUT THE TEAM

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and are dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



Our Core Values:



DELIGHT THE CLIENT: We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.



MASTER YOUR CRAFT: We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.



FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS: When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.

We are building an exceptional place to work with strong systems and high standards, not just managing tasks for a real estate business.



ABOUT YOU

Do you think in priorities, timelines, and dependencies? Do you enjoy turning a ratified purchase contract or listing agreements into a structured project plan with clear deadlines? Are you the type of person who catches a missing signature or a math error on a Closing Disclosure (CD) before anyone else sees it? Do you take pride in building clean systems, documenting SOPs, and keeping a team aligned without needing constant supervision? Do you love to communicate quickly and help keep the project on track?

If you are a high-level operator who wants to own the "engine room" of a high-volume real estate team, keep reading.



RESPONSIBILITIES

Transaction & Listing Management

Contract-to-Close Ownership: Manage every aspect of buyer and seller transactions from signed purchase agreement to closing using Skyslope and GoHighLevel.

Offer Packet Coordination: Prepare an offer packet draft with precise signature blocks and routing, and submit to the broker for review. 



Compliance & Auditing: Submit all required documentation to the office broker; ensure 100% file compliance at direction of the responsible broker by monitoring for no missing disclosures.

Closing Logistics: Coordinate with Attorneys, Lenders, Title officers and brokers; collect CD/ALTA statements for broker accuracy review against the commission agreement.



Project & Operations Management

System Building: Spot inefficiencies in the workflow and design/implement improvements or automations to reduce manual effort.



SOP Documentation: Document workflows and create step-by-step guides so the process is repeatable and scalable.

KPI Reporting: Maintain and update the "Deal Tracker" G-Sheet and GHL Opportunity values to ensure leadership has accurate data at all times.



The "Connective Tissue": Act as the bridge between leadership, agents, and clients to ensure no ball is ever dropped.



REQUIREMENTS



Experience & Capability



1. Must have extensive experience in real estate transaction coordination, transaction management, or as a high-level operations associate.

2. Preferred BPO Experience: Previous background in BPO Customer Service is highly preferred.

3. Project Management Experience: Comfortable managing 20+ active files simultaneously with varying deadlines.

4. Automation Mindset: Experience automating workflows (or a deep understanding of how to design them) is highly preferred.

5. Technical Proficiency: Working knowledge of Skyslope and GoHighLevel (GHL) is strongly preferred but not required. Fluent in AI tools (Gemini/Claude/OpenClaw) to improve output speed.



Work Style & Standards

Extremely Detail-Oriented: You find the needle in the haystack every time.

Near-Native English: Exceptional written and spoken communication skills.

Proactive Ownership: You don’t wait to be told something is broken; you anticipate it and fix it before it breaks.

Speed & Urgency: You move with a sense of purpose and meet every deadline, no excuses.



Technical Requirements

A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION is required (at least 150mbps).

Must be available to respond to messages within a 12-hour daily window (standard core hours 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM EST).

Must be flexible to work 2–4 hours on weekends as needed for urgent business requirements



APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS



STEP 1: Application

Please submit your application HERE 

To ensure you have read through the requirements, please write the phrase "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field of the application.



STEP 2: Initial Interview

If your application indicates you might be a great fit, you will be invited to participate in a quick, 10- to 15-minute initial screening interview.



STEP 3: Case Interview

Candidates who pass the initial screening will be invited to a 1-hour case interview. A case interview is an interactive session where we present you with a real-world scenario or business challenge relevant to the role. Rather than just talking about your past experience, this allows us to see your problem-solving process, technical logic, and how you tackle practical situations in real-time.



STEP 4: Reference Check

This is the final step before an official offer is extended. The final candidate(s) must provide one former employer/lead reference and one character reference.



STEP 5: The 90-Day Trial Period

If an offer is made and accepted, your employment will begin with a 90-day PAID trial period. This period is dedicated to ensuring a strong mutual fit—both for the responsibilities of the role and alignment with our core values. 

During these 90 days, we will provide you with all the necessary training, onboarding, and support you need to succeed and thrive on our team. You will be expected to master the Skyslope/GHL workflow, meet all transaction deadlines, and begin documenting/improving our internal systems. A successful trial results in a permanent invitation to the team.



Please submit your application HERE >>

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Day Shift
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $20-40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Short Form Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Google Ads Media Buyer for E-Commerce Brands ($50K–$100K+/Month Budgets)
description:
We are looking for an experienced Google Ads Media Buyer to manage and scale campaigns for e-commerce brands.



This role is for someone who has real experience managing large budgets and driving profitable growth. You must have experience managing at least $50,000–$100,000+ per month in Google Ads spend for e-commerce brands.



This will start with one project for now, with the opportunity to manage additional projects as we grow.



This is a long-term opportunity with consistent work and growth.



Responsibilities



• Manage and optimize Google Ads campaigns across Search, Shopping, Performance Max, and YouTube

• Handle monthly budgets of $50,000–$100,000+

• Improve ROAS, CPA, and overall account performance

• Scale profitable campaigns and reduce wasted spend

• Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis

• Optimize Shopping feeds and campaign structure

• Monitor performance daily and make ongoing optimizations



Requirements



• Proven experience managing Google Ads for e-commerce brands

• Experience managing $50K–$100K+/month in ad spend

• Strong experience with Shopping and Performance Max campaigns

• Strong understanding of ROAS, CPA, and scaling strategies

• Detail-oriented and reliable

• Strong English communication



Nice to Have



• Experience with Shopify brands

• Agency experience

• Experience with Meta Ads



Position Details



• Starts with one project, with opportunity for more as we grow

• Long-term opportunity

• Competitive pay based on experience



To apply, please include your experience, monthly budgets managed, example results, and e-commerce brands you have worked with. When you reply to this post, add the word "grape" at the end of the title of your reply.

title: Meta Ads Specialist – B2B Lead Generation (Marketing Agency)
description:
We’re a growth-focused marketing agency looking for a Meta Ads Specialist with hands-on experience running
 and Instagram ad campaigns for B2B lead generation. This role is focused on driving qualified leads to landing page funnels for service-based businesses, not e-commerce.



We are currently running Meta ads, and performance is not where it needs to be. We’re looking for someone who can diagnose what’s not working and clearly outline how to improve results.



You will own campaigns end-to-end, from strategy and setup to optimization and reporting, with a strong emphasis on performance, cost efficiency, and lead quality.



Key Responsibilities

• Plan, build, and manage Meta 
 & Instagram) ad campaigns for B2B lead generation

• Audit existing campaigns and funnels to identify performance issues and improvement opportunities

• Drive traffic to landing page funnels (not instant forms only) and optimize for conversions

• Develop, test, and iterate ad creatives (copy, angles, hooks) in collaboration with the creative team

• Set up and manage audiences, including cold, retargeting, and lookalikes

• Monitor daily performance metrics (CPL, CTR, CVR, spend pacing, lead quality)

• Optimize campaigns through creative testing, audience refinement, and budget adjustments

• Ensure proper tracking and attribution (Meta Pixel, CAPI, GA, CRM integrations)

• Provide clear reporting, insights, and recommendations based on performance data



Required Experience

• 2+ years managing Meta Ads for B2B lead generation

• Proven experience running ads to landing page funnels for service-based businesses or agencies

• Strong understanding of funnel-based lead gen 

• Demonstrated ability to audit and turn around underperforming ad accounts

• Experience optimizing for CPL and lead quality, not just lead volume



Audit as Part of the Hiring Process

As part of the interview process, qualified candidates will be asked to complete a short audit of our current Meta ad setup to demonstrate how they think and approach optimization.



Compensation & Growth Opportunity

• Remote role

• Contract or full-time depending on fit

• Compensation is flexible and based on experience

• There is upside for strong performance and the opportunity to earn more over time



How to Apply

Please include:

• A brief overview of your Meta Ads experience

• Examples of B2B lead gen campaigns you’ve managed (industries, budgets, results)

• If you read the entire post, include the word “banana” somewhere in your reply

title: Real Estate Virtual Assistant
description:
Hi, my name is Cameron. I’m a real estate agent based in Florida, and I run a fast-paced, growing business. I’m looking to hire a part time Real Estate Virtual Assistant who is highly organized, detail-oriented, and dependable. This is a long-term role with room to grow.



Introduction Video -  



You’ll be helping manage the backend of my business so I can stay focused on clients and growth. If you’re someone who takes initiative, thrives with structure, and enjoys supporting a team behind the scenes, this could be a great fit for you.



Communication Expectations

I value open and efficient communication. You can reach me via
, text, call, or WhatsApp, whichever is most convenient. I would like to receive a weekly
 update every Monday to review the previous week’s accomplishments and activities. Additionally, we will meet for 15-20 minutes every Monday to review your prior weeks performance, answer any questions and discuss upcoming tasks and priorities.



Working Hours

It is important that work is conducted during business hours (9AM - 5PM Eastern Time M-F) to ensure alignment with client and business needs.



Our Values and Mission

Integrity, honesty, professionalism, problem-solving, knowledge, and a commitment to excellence are the values that drive this business. Our mission is to provide exceptional real estate services that address the unique needs and concerns of each client, ensuring smooth and successful transactions through professionalism, responsiveness, and strong advocacy.



At the same time, we believe in taking care of each other as much as we care for our clients. This business represents three years of dedication, hard work, and passion, and your role will be instrumental in helping us continue to deliver exceptional service and grow to new heights.





Core Responsibilities

This role includes, but is not limited to:



Data Entry 



Sending weekly seller updates and collecting showing feedback

 

Scheduling and coordinating open houses



Marketing & Content Support



Managing Instagram,
, and YouTube uploads

Creating Canva flyers using provided templates



Clipping short-form videos from long-form content (training provided)



Ordering marketing materials, closing gifts, and print items



Calendar & Communication Management



Maintaining clean records and tracking communications



Admin & Back-End Support



Paying invoices and organizing receipts



Assisting with QuickBooks (training available if needed)



Organizing digital files and documents (Google Drive, AppFiles)



Supporting automation setup via Zapier and system improvement



Tools You'll Be Using

Follow Up Boss (CRM)



Canva



QuickBooks



Google Workspace (Drive, Docs, Sheets, Calendar)



Asana



Slack



Zoom



Loom



Vulcan7, Fello, Constant Contact



ShowingTime



Screenshot Monitor



RPR & Florida MLS systems



What I'm Looking For

Strong written and verbal English communication



1+ year experience as a VA or administrative assistant (real estate is a plus)



Proficiency with tools like Canva, Google Suite, and Slack



Attention-to-detail, Highly organized, reliable, and proactive



Ability to problem-solve independently and meet deadlines



Comfortable working during U.S. business hours



Coachable and committed to long-term growth



Bonus If You Have Experience With

MLS input (especially in Florida markets)



Real estate marketing



Zapier or workflow automation



QuickBooks or expense tracking



How to Apply



If this sounds like a good fit, please complete the application using the link provided. Please specifically mention this is for the Florida Real Estate VA Position. We will only review applicants who follow all instructions and complete the full form. Selected applicants may be asked to complete a short paid trial task and a Zoom interview.

title: Shopify Website Specialist - Project Based
description:
Project Scope: 

We are looking for someone who can quickly and efficiently:

- Update the Shopify template to a newer, on-trend look

- Create homepage banners and featured sections that link with Shopify

- Upload and organize products

- Optimize product pages (descriptions, layout, formatting)

- Create or refine collections

- Ensure the site reflects current promotions and campaigns

- Improve overall user experience (mobile + desktop)

- Identify and fix small issues (layout, spacing, broken links, etc.)



What We’re Looking For:

- Proven experience working with Shopify websites (required)

- Strong understanding of eCommerce best practices

- Experience with fashion or retail brands (highly preferred)

- Ability to work quickly without sacrificing quality

- Strong attention to detail

- Good English communication

- Ability to follow directions AND suggest improvements



Ideal Candidate:

- Has worked on live Shopify stores (not just test/demo sites)

- Understands how to make a site feel on-brand and conversion-focused

- Comfortable making updates without constant supervision

- Can spot issues and proactively fix them



Available for quick turnaround: Goal to launch the new site within 30-days from hire. 



Deliverables:

1. Updated and optimized the Shopify website aligned with the current brand direction

2. Clean, functional, and mobile-friendly pages

3. Clear communication on what was completed and any recommendations



Opportunity for Ongoing Work:

If the project goes well, we are open to continuing with:

 - Hourly support for weekly/monthly updates

- Ongoing product uploads and merchandising

- Campaign and promotion updates



How to Apply (Required)

Please include:

1. A short introduction

2. Links to Shopify websites you have worked on (REQUIRED)

3. A brief description of what you specifically did on each site

4. Your experience with fashion or eCommerce brands (if applicable)



Your hourly rate (USD) and/or project rate - $400–$800 based on experience



Your availability and estimated turnaround time - Can you commit to a 30-day turnaround?



Applications without Shopify examples will not be considered.



Bonus (Optional but Strongly Recommended)

Briefly answer:

After visiting our website, 
, what are 2–3 things you would change first? 



About Prestige:

Prestige is a Los Angeles–based women’s fashion brand, family-owned and operating since 2006. We specialize in trend-forward, fast-moving styles and are continuously updating our online store to reflect new arrivals, promotions, and seasonal campaigns.



We’re looking for a skilled Shopify Website Specialist to support a focused website update project with a fast but detail-oriented turnaround—with the opportunity for ongoing work.



Company: Prestige

Website: 

Industry: Women’s Fast Fashion (Los Angeles–based)

Founded: 2006 (Family-run business)

title: Marketing/Sales/Advertising for a hostel brand
description:
I have never hired someone for marketing, so I am trying something new to see if there is an ROI:



I operate PodShare, a shared housing hospitality concept with multiple California locations. I am looking for someone highly proactive, analytical, and execution-focused who can help increase bookings and partnerships.



This is not a passive content role. I am looking for someone who takes ownership of results and is obsessed with improving occupancy.

 

I am looking for someone to 



1) Create lists of schools, organizations, non profits, wedding planners, event promotoers etc. to cold call and setup tours of our facility, buyout for sports teams, pitch intern housing - with follow ups by
 and social media messages.



2) write engaging content, take our photos and use them with .ai to publish posts daily and newsletters 2x a month



3) have knowledge to setup Pixel on
,, Zappier, Notion, and other tools for recapture/marketing



4) be obsessed with our daily occupancies, and present me with stats daily and how you are moving the needles on bookings



5) using our past guests for re-targeting messages, and organizing folks for area specific or age specific messaging. 



6) engage influencers for "trades" to stay a night for a post - even for 3K followers so long as they have real engagement and not bots then they have a small base. 



7) Optimize OTA listings (Airbnb, 
, Hostelworld) to improve conversion rates and create blog posts on our website for SEO.



8. Improve Google Business Profiles with posts, photos, and responses to reviews.



I am not looking for a novice -- your first task with me will be to setup
 Pixel to our booking engine. This will require knowledge but also trouble shooting w/ our PMS so I am looking for someone with technical marketing knowledge and logic/independence to take a task all the way.



I also would like you to setup Zappier to Notify us when occupancy is low ? trigger promotions



Ideal candidate



Highly resourceful and independent

Comfortable troubleshooting technical setups

Strong written communication

Understands marketing funnels

Experience with hospitality or travel is a plus

Experience with Zapier, Meta Pixel, CRM tools preferred



RATE: $3/hour

HOURS: 30 hours per week

Schedule: Flexible but something like:



Mon–Fri

8am–11am Pacific ? outreach & partnerships

5pm–7pm Pacific ? social posting, engagement, monitoring bookings



Sat: 2–4 hours flexible ? content scheduling, influencer outreach, reports

Total ? 30hrs/week



Example schedule:



weekly output targets: outreach:

40–80 new contacts added

30–50 outreach messages sent

5–10 follow-ups



content:

5–7 posts per week

1–2 reels



influencers:



10 outreach messages per week

1–2 confirmed stays per month



:

2 newsletters per month



automation:

1 new optimization per week

title: (RPE) Video Editors for a Canadian YouTube channel with over 400k subscribers
description:
I’m looking to hire 5 editors to join my team and help edit YouTube videos for my channel about internet documentaries. We will post videos similar to this channel [
/watch?v=jld3LLODQvQ]. There may also be opportunities to edit for my other channels if we’re happy with your performance.

About the job:

- You will be editing at least 1 video per week (maximum 3/week)

- The pay rate will be based on length of voiceover (usually 12-17minutes)

- Pay will be round $5 max per VoiceOver minute, according to the quality of the trial sample (example: 15minutes of voiceover x $5 = $75 total payout)

- You will be working with me or my managers directly so that eventually you’ll match the channel’s style perfectly.

title: Customer Support Specialist Application – Fashion E-commerce
description:
Customer Support Specialist Application – Fashion E-commerce



Thank you for your interest in joining our customer support team. We are a fast-growing fashion e-commerce brand and are looking for an experienced Customer Support Specialist who can handle high volumes of inquiries while maintaining a friendly, professional tone.



Role Overview

As part of our support team, you will be responsible for handling customer inquiries via Zendesk, assisting with order-related questions, managing returns and refunds, and ensuring a smooth customer experience. You will also communicate internally via 
 and occasionally coordinate with suppliers when needed.



Required Experience



* Strong experience with Zendesk (ticket handling, macros, automation)

* Good understanding of Shopify (orders, tracking, product info, customer data)

* Experience in fashion e-commerce and/or dropshipping stores

* Comfortable working in a remote team environment using 

* Ability to handle high ticket volumes efficiently



Application Questions



Please answer the following questions in detail:



1. Zendesk Experience

   Are you familiar with Zendesk?

   After responding to a customer
, do you usually set the ticket to Open, Pending, or Solved? Please explain your workflow.



2. Shopify Knowledge

   How well are you familiar with Shopify?

   What kind of tasks have you handled within Shopify (e.g., order management, refunds, tracking, product info)?



3. 
 Communication

   Our team communicates entirely through 
, including supplier contact.

   Are you familiar with 
, and have you used it in a work environment before?



4. Dropshipping / Fashion Experience

   With how many fashion or dropshipping stores have you worked?

   If possible, please share reference websites or brands you have supported.



5. Current Work Situation

   Are you currently working as a customer support agent for other stores?

   If not, why did you stop working with your previous employer?



6. Return Handling (Practical Question)

   What would your response be to a customer who wants to return a fashion item?

   Please write out the exact message you would send.



7. Wrong Size Scenario (Practical Question)

   What would your response be to a customer who says the size they ordered does not fit correctly?

   Please write out the exact message you would send.

title: Property Ops Lead – Guest Experience + Refurb Coordination
description:
We are a UK-based property investment and serviced accommodation (SA) management company. We operate seven serviced accommodation units and focus on delivering a consistent, high-standard guest experience through strong operational systems and quality control. Alongside SA operations, we run two to three active refurbishment/development projects at any given time, coordinating contractors, procurement, documentation, and stakeholder communication to deliver projects efficiently and to a high standard.



This is a hands-on Operations Lead role responsible for execution across our serviced accommodation portfolio and coordination support across refurbishment/development projects. Your core focus is proactive quality control and consistent delivery: you’ll enforce standards through checklists and evidence, ensure guests have a smooth end-to-end experience, and reduce recurring issues by continuously improving SOPs. In parallel, you’ll support project coordination for refurbishments by running follow-ups with contractors and suppliers, managing documentation, and scheduling appointments to keep workstreams moving without drift.



We’re hiring an Operations Lead who can run a cadence, enforce quality standards, and coordinate delivery across both serviced accommodation and active refurb projects.



Monday- Sunday:10:00-18:00 ( with 1 hour break) UK time, Saturday or Sunday off for one day.

Salary :700 USD Per month+ KPI bonus



Core responsibilities

Serviced Accommodation operations (delivery + standards)

- Enforce unit standards using checklists, photo evidence, and “definition of done” quality bars.

- Manage guest communications: fast responses, professional tone, calm resolution, and proactive updates.

- Maintain an issues/defects log and ensure issues are fully closed with root-cause prevention (not just “ticket closed”).

- Coordinate cleaners and operational VAs: confirm turnovers, verify quality, and drive consistent performance.

- Monitor pricing and availability: check pricing rules are being applied, flag risks/opportunities, and escalate recommendations.

- Create and update listings: listing setup, copy accuracy, amenities, house rules, photo requirements, and platform compliance ( ---------- as applicable).

- Maintain and improve SOPs: update processes based on recurring issues, guest feedback, and operational learnings.

Property project coordination (admin execution)

- Maintain a project tracker for tasks, owners, due dates, and blockers.

- Source and compare quotes: produce a quote comparison table and recommendation.

- Follow-up with contractors, suppliers, utilities, council, other professional, keep reference numbers, paper trail, and next-step ownership.

- Documentation: build/format/update project packs, submissions, and version control.

- Appointment scheduling: inspections, contractor visits, deliveries, surveys, and access coordination.

Reporting directly to company CEO



Tools and working style

- We use Lark for communication, tracking, and internal workflows.

- Google Drive for document storage and document control.

- PMS/pricing tools as applicable (Hostaway/PriceLabs or similar).



Must-have experience

- Evidence-based quality control: Create and used checklists/audits/photo evidence used to enforce standards and reduce recurring issues.

- Serviced accommodation or hospitality ops: guest comms + turnover coordination + issue resolution.

- Property management working experience

- Vendor/contractor coordination: proactive follow-up, scheduling, and timeline control.

- Strong written English: crisp guest messaging and concise leadership updates.

- SOP ownership: has maintained/improved SOPs based on operational reality, not just followed them.

Nice-to-have experience

- Listing creation and optimization ( ---------- ).

- Pricing/availability monitoring and familiarity with revenue management tools (e.g., PriceLabs).

- Property management operations or refurb coordination admin.

Personality traits we’re looking for

- Conscientious and detail-accurate.

- Proactive follow-up and ownership mindset.

- Structured and process-driven (lives in checklists/trackers).

- Calm, professional, and solution-oriented with guests and vendors.

- Improves systems continuously rather than firefighting repeatedly.

- Well organised with multiple task and able to prioritise



Hiring process:

Step 1: Answer the Google link below (required) and follow the instructions after.

Step 2: Answer scenario based questions

Step 3: Join 20-minute screening call

Step 4: Paid trial task

Step 5: Final interview + references



HOW TO APPLY:



Click the link ----------

I’ll be in touch with you in 3-5 working days.



Please note, we ONLY accept applications through this application process. PLEASE DO NOT use the website’s message board as we don’t monitor this.

title: Video Marketing & Ads Growth Specialist (Real Estate Focus)
description:
The Role

I am looking for a marketing partner to turn raw video footage into lead generation. You will not only edit video but also manage Custom Audience Targeting using skip-traced property lists to ensure our ads are seen by specific high-intent homeowners and out-of-state landlords.



Responsibilities

Video Editing: Take raw phone footage and edit it into engaging YouTube Shorts and long-form videos with captions, hooks, and branding.

Data & List Management: Handle CSV uploads of skip-traced property lists (Non-Owner Occupied, Probate, etc.) into Google and
 Ads.

Custom Audience Targeting: Set up and manage Google Customer Match and
 Custom Audiences to retarget specific individuals from our lead lists.

Omni-Channel Retargeting: Ensure anyone who visits our website or appears on our "high-potential" lists sees our video ads across YouTube and social media.

YouTube SEO: Optimize video metadata to ensure we rank for local "Sell my house fast" keywords in the Salt Lake Valley.



Required Skills & Qualifications

Proficient in Video Editing: Expert in CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or DaVinci Resolve.

Ad Tech Mastery: Proven experience with Google Ads Manager, Customer Match, and Retargeting Pixels.

Data Handling: Comfortable cleaning and formatting CSV files for ad platform uploads.

Real Estate Knowledge: (Highly Preferred) Experience working with PropStream, BatchLeads, or similar REI data tools.

Language: Excellent written and verbal English skills.

Work Ethic: A "self-starter" attitude. I am looking for someone who works hard and takes ownership of the lead-gen results.



Why Work with Us?

Long-Term Growth: This is a permanent position for the right person as we scale our Salt Lake operations.

Direct Access: You will work directly with the owner, Eric.

Professional Impact: Your work will directly help local families solve difficult property problems.

title: Virtual Assistant for Film Research and Asset Sourcing for Instagram Reels
description:
I’m looking for a detail-oriented virtual assistant to help source visual assets for a recurring series of short Instagram/TikTok reels about international filmmakers, cinema history, and film culture.



For each video, you will receive a short script about a filmmaker. Your job will be to gather relevant images, article screenshots, and YouTube video clips that match the script and can be used by an editor.



This is a research-heavy role, so I’m looking for someone who is organized, fast, accurate, and interested in film, media, history, or online content.



For each reel/script, you will source:



-5 high-resolution headshots/images of the filmmaker

These should be large, clear, and usable in a video edit. No tiny, blurry, low-quality images.



-3 relevant vertical screenshots from articles.

These should visually support the script. For example, news articles, interviews, reviews, festival pages, historical sources, or articles about the filmmaker’s work.



-5 to 8 relevant video clips from YouTube

You will provide YouTube links, timestamps, and a short note explaining why each clip is useful. These could include interviews, behind-the-scenes clips, film excerpts, archival footage, trailers, or public talks.



Organize everything clearly

-You will place all links, notes, and files into a Google Drive folder and/or tracking spreadsheet so the editor can easily use them.



You are a good fit if you:



Have strong internet research skills

Can find high-quality images and useful video clips quickly

Can follow a script and understand what visuals would support each sentence

Are organized and reliable

Have good English reading comprehension

Are interested in film, filmmakers, documentaries, history, art, or culture

Know how to use Google Drive, Google Sheets, and YouTube

Can avoid irrelevant, low-quality, or incorrect assets



Experience with editing, content creation, film research, or social media is a plus, but not required.



PAY:



Pay is $5 USD per video, with each video expected to take around one hour.



We will start with a paid test assignment. If the test goes well, the first batch will likely be 20 videos, with potential for ongoing work after that.



How to Apply



Please reply with:



A short paragraph about your research experience

Any experience you have with film, YouTube, social media, editing, or content research

Your internet speed and availability

A sample of a filmmaker, artist, or public figure you’ve researched before, if applicable

Put the phrase FILM RESEARCH VA at the top of your application so I know you read the full post



Looking for someone detail-oriented, reliable, and excited to help build a high-quality film education series.

==============================



We’re looking for a visual storyteller who knows how to stop the scroll.

The "Living in Charlotte" team is growing, and we need a designer who understands that a thumbnail isn't just a "pretty picture"—it’s the front door to our business. We’re looking for someone who obsesses over click-through rates (CTR), understands human psychology, and can turn a real estate topic into a "must-click" visual.



If you’re the type of person who spends your free time analyzing why certain creators dominate the homepage, we want to talk to you.





IMPORTANT: Please read this entire listing. We’re big on the details here. The final step of our process involves a portfolio review and a reference check from a former client or team lead.



==============

ABOUT THE TEAM

===============





We operate like a fast-paced startup. We’re high-energy, high-standard, and we care deeply about the people we serve. If you have a growth mindset and love seeing your work directly impact a channel's success, you’ll fit right in.



What’s it like here? Check out this video to meet the crew. 



=============

Our Core Values:

=============



Delight the Client: We go beyond "good enough" to create visual 
nts that stick.



Master Your Craft: You aren't just using templates. You’re staying ahead of design trends and YouTube's ever-changing algorithm.



Follow Through, Always: Deadlines matter. When a video is ready to go live, the thumbnail needs to be ready to perform.





==========

ABOUT YOU

==========





Do you look at a boring Thumbnail and immediately see three different ways to make it visual? Do you understand how to use color, contrast, and facial expressions to create curiosity? Are you the person who catches a weird shadow or a messy crop that everyone else missed?

We need a high-level creative who wants to own the "packaging" of our brand. You should be comfortable taking raw ideas and turning them into high-performing assets without needing someone to hold your hand through the process.



====================

WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

====================





Design & Strategy



The "Hook": Create 3 - 7 main thumbnails with different variations. High-performing thumbnails per week that drive clicks.



Psychology-Based Design: Use depth, lighting, and "the gap" (creating a question only the video can answer) to increase CTR.



A/B Testing: Design variations to see which colors or layouts the audience prefers.

Photo Editing: Clean up property photos, color-grade portraits, and make every asset look premium.







Operations & Growth



Performance Tracking: Look at the data. If a thumbnail isn't clicking, you’re the first to suggest a pivot.

Asset Management: Keep our library of headshots and Charlotte-specific b-roll organized and ready to use.

Collaboration: Jump into brainstorming sessions to help us title videos before we film them so the visuals and words work together perfectly.





======================

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

======================



A Killer Portfolio: You’ve done this before. You have examples of thumbnails that actually worked.



The Tools: You’re a pro in Photoshop or similar high-end design software. You also know how to use AI (like Midjourney or Generative Fill) to speed up your workflow, not replace your creativity.



Speed & Communication: We move fast. You need to be able to handle revisions quickly and keep us updated on Slack.



Charlotte Vibes: You don't have to live here, but you need to be able to capture the "vibe" of our city through your designs.



============

HOW TO APPLY

============





STEP 1: The Application Submit your info 
. 



To show us you’re paying attention to the details, please include the phrase "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.



STEP 2: Quick Chat If your portfolio blows us away, we’ll hop on a 15-minute intro call.



STEP 3: The Design Challenge We’ll give you a title and some raw assets and ask you to show us your magic. This is a paid trial task.



STEP 4: Reference Check We’ll chat with one person you’ve worked with in the past.



STEP 5: 90-Day Trial If we’re a match, we’ll start with a 90-day trial period. We’ll give you the training and support you need to master our systems. If you crush it, you’ll be a permanent part of the team.





Application form here

title: Project-Based Real Estate Video Editor
description:
WHO WE ARE



Rex Visual Services is a real estate media company based in Atlanta, Georgia. We specialize in providing cutting-edge visual solutions to real estate professionals, including high-quality photography, video tours, and a comprehensive suite of media marketing materials to elevate their online presence.



HOW WILL YOU CONTRIBUTE



We are in search of a skilled and creative Real Estate Video Editor to join our team. As a freelance editor for Rex Visual Services, you will be responsible for creating compelling and visually engaging video content that aligns with our media services. You’ll handle standard profile and log footage, as well as both ground-level and aerial footage, delivering a range of video types, such as:



• Home-tour highlight videos

• Agent home walkthroughs (on/off-camera)

• Closing day reels

• Client testimonials

• Realtors' event recaps

• Interview-style videos for social media

• Short-form content tailored for platforms such as Instagram Reels, TikTok, and YouTube Shorts



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING



• Coordinate with the Team Manager to follow project requirements, objectives, and target delivery.

• Meet project deadlines and manage editing projects simultaneously, as needed.

• Submit edited footage to the Team Manager for review and approval.

• Incorporate feedback and finalize the video with any necessary revisions.

• Collaborate with the team to brainstorm and contribute innovative ideas to develop our video content strategy.



COMPENSATION & WORK SETUP



• This is a remote (WFH) position offering a rate of $40-$90 per project, with the expectation to turnaround 1-2 videos a day.

• Work hours will be from 2 PM-10 PM, Monday-Friday (weekends if needed), Philippine Time.

• Potential for a full-time role based on performance.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS



We’re looking for someone who is friendly, organized, creative, and able to communicate well with our team. Please place the word "orange" in the subject line of your
. In terms of skills, we are seeking someone with the following:



• Proven work experience as a Video Editor or similar role.

• A portfolio or demo reel showcasing previous video editing works is highly desirable.

• Proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro (Adobe After Effects skills would be a plus). Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve can also be considered.

• Must have a computer system with technical specifications that can handle 1080p up to 4K footage and render projects with motion graphics & animation.

• Sufficient English skills to read, understand, and craft appropriate responses to our team.

• Familiarity with a Project Management System like Tonomo, Dropbox, and a Communication App such as Slack is also preferred, but not mandatory.



You can also find examples here of the type of work we’d be looking for you to produce:

» 



Project-Based Payment Guidelines:

» 



READY TO JOIN?



To apply, please
 
 with your resumé and portfolio, along with any relevant work samples and experience you may have. A portfolio is a MUST. We can't wait to see what you can bring to the table!

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: Direct Response Video Editor (Meta Ads)
description:
Performance Video Editor (Meta Ads) – ELYVIA Skincare

Are you a Meta Ads expert who can blend cinematic B-roll storytelling, traditional UGC, and AI-assisted content into high-converting skincare creatives?



We are building ELYVIA, a premium UK-based skincare brand focused on innovative at-home infusion treatments. We don’t just sell products; we sell a new biological belief through education and high-impact direct-response marketing.



We are looking for a Creative Editor who understands that a winning ad is a mix of tight psychology, visual retention, and the ability to "show, not just tell."



ABOUT THE ROLE

This is not a basic "cut-and-paste" role. You will be responsible for turning detailed briefings into polished Meta ads. We need an editor who is a master of versatility:



Master the Scroll-Stop: Craft hooks that immediately stop our target audience (Women 40+) in their tracks using high-quality B-roll or UGC.



Versatile Content Creation: You must be able to build powerful ads using a mix of traditional UGC, cinematic B-roll, and AI-generated avatars/UGC when the script requires it.



Visualize the Mechanism: Use B-roll and animations to explain how ELYVIA  works.



Maintain ELYVIA Branding: Every ad must feel premium and credible. We want u to be able to make branded ads.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

Direct Response Editing: Create high-impact 9:16 video ads (VSL-style) focused entirely on performance and conversion.



Strategic Sourcing: We provide a base set of assets (B-roll & VO), but we expect you to source your own additional visuals to perfectly match the narrative and fill any gaps. 



All-Round Footage Handling: Whether it’s raw customer UGC, professional product shots, or AI-generated footage, you can weave it into a seamless story. 



Mechanism Animations: Source or create clean visuals that show our products, the more branded the better.



THE WORKFLOW & PAID TEST

We believe in clear direction but highly value own creativity. For every project, you will receive:



A Written Script (the narrative flow).



A Visual Briefing (Istructions on which visuals you can bind to the script).



A Base set of B-roll and assets.



Paid Test:

We start with a paid trial video. Since our scripts are comprehensive and focused on deep education, the test video will be over 60 seconds.



Fixed Test Fee: $10



REQUIRED EXPERIENCE

Proven experience editing Meta Ads, VSLs, or performance-driven UGC.



Expertise in sourcing high-quality B-roll that fits specific psychological triggers.



Proficiency in software like Premiere Pro, After Effects, DaVinci, or CapCut Pro.



Experience with AI video tools / AI UGC.



Ability to work independently and bring your own creative "flair" to a briefing.



We are specifically looking for someone who understands:



how to make the first 3 seconds scroll-stopping

how to keep visual pacing tight

how to make scripts easy to follow visually

how to source relevant B-roll

how to create visual contrast between “problem” and “solution”

how to edit before/after proof sections

how to use animations for mechanism explanation

how to make skincare ads look premium and credible

how to edit for Meta ads, not YouTube or long-form content



COMPENSATION & GROWTH

We are looking for a long-term partner, not a one-off freelancer.



Ongoing: Consistent weely workload as we scale.



Growth: Potential to grow into a Lead Editor role with performance-based bonuses for "Winning Creatives."



HOW TO APPLY

Send your Portfolio.



Which editing software do you use?



Ready to help us define the future of premium skincare? Apply now.



Kind regards,



David

title: Senior Assistant Property Manager
description:
Senior Assistant Property Manager

Aoede LLC | 100% Remote | Full-Time



About the Role



The Senior Assistant Property Manager is a leadership role responsible for overseeing the day-to-day operations of a growing, diversified real estate portfolio across Texas and California. This includes a 70-unit commercial building in Houston, TX, single-family rental properties in California, and short-term rental (Airbnb) properties in Austin, TX.



You will lead and supervise a team of Assistant Property Managers while personally handling complex operational, financial, and tenant matters. This is not an entry-level position — we need someone who has managed US properties, can make decisions independently, and is ready to own outcomes.



This is also not a role where you simply delegate and disappear. You need to be hands-on, involved in ongoing tasks, and ready to step in with guidance when a tea
mber needs it — or take over a task entirely when the situation calls for it. Our leadership philosophy is trust but verify: you empower your team to execute, then you follow up and confirm the work was done correctly.



This is a 100% remote, full-time, long-term position.



We do annual team bonding trips in the Philippines — if you're talented, hardworking, and ambitious, we'd love to meet you in person and talk about where your career is headed.



What You'll Do



Team Leadership

- Supervise and mentor Assistant Property Managers using a trust-but-verify approach — delegate clearly, follow up consistently, and confirm quality

- Stay hands-on with ongoing tasks — review work in progress, not just final outputs

- Step in to provide guidance, coaching, or direct support when tea
mbers are stuck or struggling

- Take over tasks when the situation requires it — no task is beneath you

- Apply project management principles to plan, track, and deliver property operations initiatives on time and within scope

- Schedule tea
etings, delegate tasks, and hold tea
mbers accountable for deadlines and quality

- Conduct training sessions using company runbooks and tutorials

- Serve as the first point of escalation for the team before matters reach ownership



Property Operations

- Oversee daily operations across a mixed portfolio of commercial, single-family, and short-term rental properties

- Ensure excellent customer service to all tenants, guests, and prospects

- Manage Airbnb listings, guest communications, and short-term rental operations

- Coordinate maintenance requests and vendor communication across multiple property types

- Ensure accuracy and timely preparation of all required reports



Tenant Relations & Legal

- Handle escalated tenant situations — complaints, disputes, and difficult conversations

- Prepare and serve legal notices (pay or quit, lease violations, cure or quit)

- Coordinate eviction processes with legal counsel and local authorities

- Ensure compliance with US property management regulations including Section 8, rent control, and tenant/landlord laws in California and Texas



Financial Management

- Participate in budget planning for properties across the portfolio

- Negotiate vendor contracts and pricing to optimize operational costs

- Set and review lease pricing based on market conditions

- Enter accounts receivable and accounts payable into QuickBooks

- Perform monthly bank reconciliations

- Monitor rent collections, track arrears, and escalate delinquencies



Documentation & Communication

- Draft lease agreements and manage property documentation

- Prepare and send communication notices to tenants

- Coordinate via Zoom, Slack, and Asana daily



What We're Looking For



- 2+ years of US property management experience (required — non-negotiable)

- Proven experience managing commercial, residential, or short-term rental properties in the US

- Understanding of Project Management (PMP) principles — you should be comfortable with scope management, task prioritization, risk identification, stakeholder communication, and delivering results on schedule. PMP certification is a plus but not required — what matters is that you think and operate like a project manager

- Experience leading and managing tea
mbers — you must have real examples of how you've delegated work, verified completion, coached tea
mbers through challenges, and stepped in to take over when needed

- A hands-on leadership style — you don't just assign tasks and walk away, you stay involved and ensure quality at every step

- Experience handling tenant escalations, legal notices, and eviction coordination

- Understanding of US property management regulations — Section 8, rent control, fair housing, and state-level tenant/landlord laws in California and Texas

- Proficiency in QuickBooks

- Expert-level use of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)

- Familiarity with property management software (AppFolio strongly preferred)

- Comfortable using Asana, Slack, and Zoom for daily communication

- Strong written and spoken English — you will be communicating directly with US tenants, vendors, and legal contacts

- Financially literate — comfortable with budgets, vendor negotiations, and lease pricing decisions

- Bachelor's degree preferred

- Dependable, proactive, and able to make sound decisions without constant oversight

- Must provide 3 work references from US-based individuals who can verify your US property management experience (this is required and will be checked before any offer is made)



What We Offer



- 100% remote position — work from home

- Long-term role with real growth potential into senior management

- On-the-spot bonuses for high performers

- Raises considered at performance evaluations for those who master the training material and execute at a high level

- Extensive runbooks and tutorials to support your development

- Annual team bonding trips in the Philippines

- A team culture that values hard work, initiative, and continuous learning



---



Our Hiring Process



1. Apply — Submit your application with the details listed below

2. Initial Review — We review your experience and qualifications

3. Assessment — Qualified candidates receive a TestGorilla skills assessment (~1 hour, webcam required)

4. Interview — Candidates who score at or above the 50th percentile are invited to a Zoom interview

5. Reference Check — We contact your 3 US-based references to verify your property management experience

6. Offer & Onboarding — Successful candidates begin with a 3-month probation period with full training and support



---



Frequently Asked Questions



What types of properties will I manage?

You'll oversee a diversified portfolio that includes a 70-unit commercial building in Houston, single-family rental homes in California, and short-term rental (Airbnb) properties in Austin. You'll also lead the team responsible for day-to-day management of these properties.



How is this different from the Assistant Property Manager role?

The Senior APM leads and supervises the APM team, handles escalated tenant situations and legal processes, and is involved in financial decisions including budgeting, vendor negotiations, and lease pricing. You are expected to operate independently and make sound decisions.



What leadership style are you looking for?

We believe in trust but verify. You delegate tasks to your team, trust them to execute, and then follow up to confirm the work was done correctly. But you're not just a manager — you're hands-on. You stay involved in ongoing tasks, provide guidance when tea
mbers need it, and take over tasks yourself when the situation requires it. If you're the type of leader who only gives orders from a distance, this role isn't for you.



Do I need a PMP certification?

PMP certification is a plus but not required. What we care about is that you understand and apply project management principles in your daily work — scoping tasks clearly, prioritizing effectively, identifying risks before they become problems, communicating proactively with stakeholders, and delivering results on time. If you naturally think this way, you'll thrive here.



What are the working hours?

You will be expected to work US Central Time business hours. This is a full-time position (40 hours/week).



How and when do I get paid?

Pay is on the 1st and 15th of each month via Wise (
).



Is this freelance or full-time?

This is a full-time, dedicated position. Freelancing or side work is not allowed. We need your full commitment.



Do you provide equipment?

You must have your own laptop, headset, and reliable high-speed internet connection.



Is there a probation period?

Yes, there is a 3-month probation period. After that, performance evaluations are conducted every 6 months.



How do raises and bonuses work?

We offer on-the-spot bonuses for exceptional work. Raises are considered at your 6-month performance evaluations based on how well you learn the training material, lead your team, and execute your responsibilities.



What training and support do you provide?

We have extensive runbooks, tutorials, and experienced tea
mbers to help you get up to speed on our specific processes. While we expect you to bring US property management knowledge, we'll train you on our systems and workflows.



What software will I need to know?

AppFolio (property management), QuickBooks (accounting), Asana (task management), Slack (communication), and Zoom (meetings). Proficiency in AppFolio and QuickBooks is strongly preferred for this role.



Why do you require US-based references?

We require 3 work references from US-based individuals to verify your US property management experience. This is non-negotiable. References should be former supervisors, clients, or colleagues who can speak to your property management work in the US. We will contact all references before extending an offer.



---



To Apply



Please include:

1. A summary of your US property management experience

2. What types of US properties you've managed (commercial, residential, short-term rental)

3. Your experience handling tenant escalations, legal notices, or evictions

4. Describe your leadership style — give a specific example of how you've delegated a task to a tea
mber, verified the work, and stepped in when guidance was needed

5. Any project management training, certifications, or experience (PMP, Agile, etc.)

6. What PM and accounting software you've used

7. 3 work references from US-based individuals (name, company, role, phone/email)

8. Your availability to start



About Aoede LLC



Aoede LLC is a Texas-based property management company overseeing a growing, diversified portfolio of commercial, single-family, and short-term rental properties across Texas and California. Founded with the mindset of a startup but built on enterprise-level professional experience, we move fast, build smart, and hold ourselves to a high standard.



Our team of 10+ professionals spans multiple countries and operates fully remote, managing everything from a 70-unit commercial building in Houston to Airbnb properties in Austin and single-family rentals in California. We hire talent from around the world — including the Philippines, Vietnam, India, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Nigeria, Kenya, Ghana, South Africa, Venezuela, and beyond. We don't care where you sit. We care how you perform.



What makes us different: we combine the speed and scrappiness of a startup with the processes, structure, and professionalism you'd expect from a much larger organization. We invest heavily in documentation — our runbooks and tutorials are built so that the right person can get up to speed quickly and operate independently. We reward performance, not tenure. If you learn the material, execute at a high level, and take ownership of your work, you'll grow here.



Our culture is built on communication, collaboration, and humility. We operate as a team — not in silos. Everyone, from leadership down, is open to feedback and willing to hear what they need to do to improve. We're not perfect and we don't pretend to be. What we do is strive for continuous improvement and learning every single day. Leadership is genuinely invested in finding better ways to do things, and we expect the same mindset from everyone on the team.



A Note on Honesty



We value honesty over impressive claims. Please don't tell us you're an expert in something you've only read about or briefly touched. It's okay to say "I've used AppFolio a few times but I'm not an expert" or "I haven't handled evictions before but I'm willing to learn." We will find out the truth — whether it's during the assessment, the interview, or the first week on the job. And when we do, it damages trust that's very hard to rebuild.



We would much rather hire someone who says "I don't know that yet, but I'll learn it" than someone who claims to know everything and can't deliver. Being humble, honest about where you are, and willing to put in the work to grow — that's what gets you far here. We have the training, the runbooks, and the team support to get the right person up to speed. What we can't teach is integrity.



We're actively expanding our portfolio and our team. We do annual team bonding trips because we believe the people behind the work matter just as much as the work itself.



This is a company where your work matters, your voice is heard, and your growth is directly tied to what you put in.

title: Video Ads Editor
description:
Novantro is a Reddit Marketing Agency helping B2B companies and agencies generate revenue from Reddit. We're founder-led, lean by design, and currently at $50K/month. Our goal is simple: hit $100K/month, and we're moving fast to get there.



Why We're Hiring



This role is for us, not our clients. We need a dedicated video editor to own all of Novantro's
 and Instagram ad creative the ads that bring new B2B clients to us.



We're looking for a video editor who lives and breathes
 and Instagram ads. This is not a general video editing role your primary focus will be cutting high-performing ad creative for Meta platforms (Feed, Reels, Stories) that stops the scroll, holds attention, and drives conversions. If you understand the difference between an ad that gets ignored and one that prints money, we want to talk to you.



What You'll Do



Edit short-form video ads (30-120 seconds) optimized for
 and Instagram placements (9:16, 1:1, 4:5).

Craft strong hooks within the first 3 seconds to maximize rate.

Add captions, text overlays, motion graphics, and sound design that work with sound off (and on).

Produce multiple variations of the same ad for A/B and creative testing (different hooks, CTAs, pacing, thumbnails).

Edit UGC-style content, talking-head videos, product demos, and mixed-format creatives.

Iterate quickly based on performance data if an ad is winning, cut more like it; if it's losing, diagnose why.

Stay current with Meta ad trends, formats, and policy requirements.



Must-Have Experience



2+ years editing video ads specifically for
 and Instagram (please share examples).

Strong portfolio of Meta ads you've edited, ideally with performance metrics (CTR, ROAS, hook rate) where possible.

Proficiency in Premiere Pro, After Effects, CapCut, or equivalent.

Deep understanding of vertical/square video, native captioning, and Meta's ad specs.

Sense for direct-response pacing fast cuts, pattern interrupts, clear CTAs.

Ability to edit and deliver ads quickly without sacrificing quality (often multiple variations per concept).



Nice to Have



Experience editing for YouTube (long-form, YouTube Shorts, or YouTube ads).

Experience working directly with media buyers or performance marketers.



What We're NOT Looking For



Cinematic/narrative editors with no paid social experience.

Editors who need a polished brief for every project you should be able to look at raw footage and a product and know what to do.



Application Priority



To be considered first, fill out this application form with your Reddit experience and examples:

title: Real Estate Lead Follow-Up & Qualification Specialist (Remote)
description:
***MUST SUBMIT DISC SCORES AND VOICE RECORDING IN ORDER TO BE CONSIDERED***

We are hiring an Inbound Real Estate Lead Follow-Up & Qualification Specialist to handle warm inbound homeowner leads from people actively searching online to sell their homes.



This role exists to filter, qualify, or disqualify leads before they ever reach our acquisition team.



This is not sales, not cold calling, and not persuasion.



If you are comfortable asking direct questions, enforcing clear criteria, and ending calls professionally when a lead is not a fit, this role may be right for you.



What You’ll Be Doing



Respond to warm inbound homeowner inquiries (no cold calling)



Conduct structured qualification calls using a clear scorecard



Ask direct questions about:



motivation



timeline



property condition



decision-maker status



price expectations



Apply qualification rules consistently



Disqualify early when criteria are not met (this is considered success)



Transfer only clean, well-qualified opportunities to acquisitions



Document outcomes clearly in the CRM



You are evaluated on decision quality and discipline, not call volume.



Work Schedule & Availability (Important)



This role requires full-day availability, not just total hours.



Typical schedule:



4–5 hours work



2-hour break



4–5 hours work



Leads come in throughout the day and require timely follow-up.



This role is not suitable if you need a fragmented or flexible schedule.



Who This Role Is For



You may be a strong fit if you:



Are comfortable asking direct questions



Can say “no” calmly and professionally



Prefer clear rules over improvisation



Stay emotionally steady on calls



Document accurately and follow SOPs



Work well independently in a remote environment



Who This Role Is NOT For



This role is likely not a fit if you:



Are looking for a sales or commission-heavy role



Prefer persuasion over qualification



Dislike enforcing rules



Want flexibility instead of structure



Feel uncomfortable disqualifying friendly sellers



Preferred DISC Tendencies (Not Required, but Helpful)



Moderate Dominance (comfortable leading conversations)



Low–moderate Influence (professional, not sales-driven)



Moderate Steadiness (calm, patient)



Moderate Compliance (respects rules and processes)



High-pressure sales personalities are not a fit.



What We Offer



100% remote role



Consistent warm inbound leads



Clear SOPs, scorecards, and expectations



Stable, long-term opportunity for the right fit



Professional, process-driven team environment



How to Apply



Please include a short answer to this question with your application:



“When would you disqualify a seller who sounds friendly but does not meet your criteria?”
